Petition For Orders: 1) Compelling Distribution; 2) Compelling The Provision Of Information; 3)Compelling Accounting; 4) Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty For Failure To Manage Trust Property To Benefit Beneficiaries, Secret Agreement For Benefit Of Trustee, And Failure To Make Property Productive; 5) Barring Payment Of Attorney'S Fees; 6) Removal Of Co-Trustees And Appointment Of Interim Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-30-2025 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Donald Sweet for petitioner Kendrick Louie (via Zoom).
Daniel Newbold for trustees Vincent Louie, Melvyn Louie, and Joyce Louie (via Zoom).

The Court noted that no response or objections had been filed. Mr. Newbold stated that a response was anticipated and that counsel had met and conferred and were attempting to resolve the issues raised in the petition. Mr. Sweet stated that the parties were exploring informal resolution, including informal discovery and the possibility of mediation, given the scope of the accounting issues.

The Court continued the matter to April 7, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
